observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to create a guideline for the grant of Assistance to Individuals in Crisis Situation (AICS) in order to aid the Municipality in the selection process of qualified beneficiaries, type and rate for each assistance, and the documentary requirements needed for the disbursement thereof, among others.
observations.managementAction: No Guideline on the AICs had been  issued by management during the year
observations.reason: The person assigned to draft the guideline overlooked the task.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to coordinate with the accountable officers for the settlement of their accountabilities.
observations.managementAction: No payment has been received from the accountable persons.
observations.reason: The Management explained that the accountable persons are already deceased, a request for write-off will be filed.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to prepare a health and safety program that enumerates safety procedures that would lessen, prevent or eliminate the effects of the identified potential hazards associated in the handling of wastes, on them and to the community.
observations.managementAction: While there was no formal training conducted, the utility workers, health workers and agricultural staff were aware of the proper waste disposal of toxic wastes by the Municipal Health Officer.
observations.reason: A Municipal Environment and Natural Resource Officer has just been appointed, and he is expected to prepare the health and safety program within the year.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 on the proper use of government vehicles and instruct the Supply Officer to properly marked with the words “For Official Use Only” (3 inches) in the agency’s owned vehicles, under which should be written the name of the Municipality of Besao, Mountain Province, to prevent unauthorized use or improper use of the vehicles.

We have recommended and Management agreed to create a specific guideline for the rental of the Municipality’s heavy equipment for proper control, assessment and evaluation on the operation of the equipment. Also, include the documentary requirements needed to serve as reference in the proper utilization of the equipment.

We have recommended and Management agreed to prepare and submit to the COA Auditor the Driver’s Trip Tickets, Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption and Official Travels, for evaluation and verification purposes, pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977, on the rules and regulations governing the use of government motor vehicles and fuel consumption.

We have recommended and Management agreed to require the contractors concerned to immediately carry out the necessary corrective measures to address the noted defects and deficiency, to prevent further deterioration and early destruction of the projects; and to avoid disallowance in audit.

We have recommended and Management agreed to compute and collect/deduct the corresponding liquidated damages on the foregoing projects from any money due to the contractors pursuant to the provisions of Item 8, Annex “E” of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. No. 9184.

We have recommended and the collectors agreed to turn over their collections daily to the Municipal Treasurer in accordance with the provisions of the Local Treasury Operations Manual; and to avoid the risk of loss and unforeseen event.

We have recommended and Management agreed to increase the fidelity bond of the Local Revenue Collection Officers, and pay its corresponding bond premium in accordance with the Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009; and in order to be able to claim from the Fidelity Fund.

We have recommended and the BAC agreed to refrain from awarding of contract to bidders who are found to be ineligible, and henceforth, award only to bidders who are eligible and complying pursuant to the provisions of the procurement process specified in the 2016 revised IRR of RA 9184, otherwise, it will be disallowed in audit.

We have recommended and Management agreed to strictly comply with the pertinent provisions on the Leave of Absence in accordance with the Omnibus Rules on Leave and Republic Act No. 7160.
We have recommended further that henceforth, Management ensures that local elective officials and employees who are absent during their sessions shall file their respective leaves of absence to be entitled to receive their salary, otherwise, payment made to them shall be disallowed in audit.

We have recommended and the Supply Officer/Property Officer agreed to tag all LGU property issued to municipal personnel with property stickers/inventory tag, for proper identification and easy monitoring, and henceforth, do the same for the succeeding issuances pursuant to the guidelines on the issuance and acknowledgement of items set forth in Chapter V, of the Training Handbook on Property and Supply Management System.
observations.managementAction: All properties of the LGU were tagged with property stickers for proper identification.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and Management agreed to henceforth, limit its obligations to the amounts appropriated for each programs, projects and activities (PPAs) pursuant to the provisions of R.A. No. 7160.

We have recommended further and Management agreed to henceforth, augment the deficient PPAs before its obligations to prevent incurrence of expenditures without valid appropriation.
observations.managementAction:
No obligations for programs, projects and activities have exceeded their respective appropriations during the year.


Augmentation for deficient PPAs during the year were made before these were obligated.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to henceforth, ensure the preparation of the Summary/ List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances and submit the same within ten working days after the end of each quarter to the NDRRMC, through the OCD and copy furnish the COA Auditors in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.
observations.managementAction: Summary list of donations received and distributed were prepared and submitted quarterly to the NDRRMC and OCD

observations.recommendation: We  have  recommended and the BAC agreed that henceforth, the BAC Secretariat prepare an Annual Procurement Plan pursuant to Section 7.2, Rule II of the RIRR of Republic Act No. 9184, and in accordance with the prescribed format (Annex A) of the GPPB Circular No. 07-2015 dated July 16, 2015.
observations.managementAction: An APP for the CY 2021 procurements have been prepared by the BAC, a copy of which was furnished to the COA Auditor

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to henceforth, require the contractors to post warranty security against defects from final acceptance of infrastructure projects pursuant to the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Warranty Security against defects from final acceptance has been required from contractors during the year.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to instruct all Accountable Officers to strictly comply with the preparation and submission of the monthly RAAF as basis for monitoring the movement of accountable forms at their possession to ensure the reliability of the monthly balances of inventory.
observations.managementAction: Monthly RAAF had been prepared and submitted to COA monthly.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed to henceforth, require business owners and organizations to submit certified photocopies of their ITRs pursuant to Item e of Section 2E.04, Article E of the Municipality’s Revenue Code.
observations.managementAction: The Treasury Office imposed the submission of certified photocopies of Income Tax Returns by businesses during the renewal of their business permits.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to collect the balances from beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction:
A total of ₱700.00 was collected for CY 2021, leaving a balance of ₱7,800.00;

Collection efforts shall continue for the remaining balance
observations.reason: Management explained that the recipients of the financial assistance were from indigent families, thus, the difficulty in the collection of the balance.
